text.title: Server vs client components and data fetching
text.summary: Server Components are the default and render on the server with no client JS shipped; Client Components add the 'use client' directive and enable hooks and event handlers.

rule.statement: Do not add 'use client' to a page or layout unless it directly uses hooks, event handlers, or browser APIs. Move the directive to the smallest child component that needs interactivity.
rule.rationale: Adding 'use client' to a page converts the entire subtree to client-rendered code, eliminating streaming and server data fetching benefits for the whole page.

example.note: Unnecessary 'use client' on a page that only needs it in one child

rule.statement: Fetch data directly in async Server Components with await. Do not route internal data through an API route. Wrap fetch/DB helper functions with React.cache() to deduplicate identical calls within the same request.
rule.rationale: Without React.cache(), two Server Components in the same render tree calling getUser(id) with the same argument make two DB round-trips. React.cache() memoizes per-request.

example.note: Client-side data fetch adds unnecessary JS and a network round-trip

rule.statement: Do not pass fetched data as props through layouts to children. Each Server Component that needs data should fetch it directly.
rule.rationale: Layouts re-render on every navigation; prop-drilling forces the layout to fetch data it does not own and creates tight coupling between parent and child components.

text.reference: Prisma client singleton for App Router
